summary: Phase 1 final foundation release: canonical release metadata, deterministic npm install, safe Vercel build order, strict generated-runtime verification and a local-first hand-model policy.
features: Release metadata is versioned from one canonical source and all runtime, cache and verifier identifiers are synchronized.
Vercel uses npm ci for deterministic dependency installation and runs the explicit vercel:build gate.
The generated MediaPipe runtime is validated after static build, including JavaScript, WASM and local model output.
The app remains runtime-local: deployed clients load MediaPipe and the hand model only from same-origin vendor paths.
The official hand model is included as a required, verified source asset in the final release package; Vercel uses the local file and does not need to download it.
No camera request is made by build, install or service-worker bootstrap.

buildPolicy.fallback: npm run model:sync is a recovery-only action; the final package already contains the verified local model
buildPolicy.runtimeNetwork: no remote MediaPipe module or model is loaded by the deployed browser
